reviews.author: Peter Zijlstra
reviews.summary: Reviewer Peter Zijlstra suggested removing the _slowpath suffix from the contended_release tracepoint declaration for consistency and to match the naming convention of other functions in the header.
reviews.raw_body:
extern for consistency with all the other declarations in this header.

s/_slowpath//, the corresponding down function also doesn't have
_slowpath on.

2026-03-04.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new tracepoint called contended_release that fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released under contention. This allows for more accurate hold time estimation and provides the holder's stack at release time, which can differ from what is captured earlier in the locking process. The new tracepoint has minimal performance impact as it only affects slowpath unlock paths.

reviews.author: Masami (Google)
reviews.summary:
Masami noted that the contended_release tracepoint instrumentation may not work correctly when the lock is released due to a signal, as the signal handler can run concurrently and interfere with the instrumentation.

Reviewer Masami suggested combining the traceevent definition and its usage in a single patch to improve understanding of the contended_release event's purpose and recording location.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed,  4 Mar 2026 16:56:15 +0000
Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:

---

OK, but I don't recommend you to split the traceevent definition
and its usage. Could you combine [1/3] and [3/3], so that we can
understand why this event is introduced and where it is recorded
by checking one commit?

Thank you,

reviews.author: Steven Rostedt
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the contended_release tracepoint instrumentation does not account for lockdep's internal locking, which can lead to false positives and requested the instrumentation be modified to exclude these cases

reviewer suggested adding a cleanup patch to remove 'extern' declarations from other functions in the series

reviewer suggested adding 'slowpath' to the description for consistency with rtmutex code
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, 4 Mar 2026 23:02:23 +0100
Peter Zijlstra <peterz@infradead.org> wrote:

---

I wonder if a cleanup patch should be added to remove the "extern" from the
other functions, as that tends to be the way things are going (hch just
recommended it elsewhere).

---

And since "slowpath" is more descriptive (and used in the rtmutex code),
should that be added too?

-- Steve

reviews.author: Steven Rostedt
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the contended_release tracepoint instrumentation may not work correctly in certain lock types, specifically percpu-rwsem, due to the lack of a slowpath unlock path, and requested additional investigation

reviewer noted that the original patch used an if statement for the contended_release tracepoint without a static branch, which can lead to performance issues when the tracepoint is disabled, and suggested using a static_branch to conditionally enable the tracepoint

reviewer expressed agreement and concurrence with the patch series
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed,  4 Mar 2026 16:56:17 +0000
Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:

---

Hmm, if statements should never be used just for tracepoints without a
static branch. The above should be:

	if (trace_contended_release_enabled() && rt_mutex_has_waiters(rtm))
		trace_contended_release(rwb);

The above "trace_contened_release_enabled()" is a static_branch where it
turns the if statement into a nop when the tracepoint is not enabled, and a
jmp when it is.

---

Same here.

-- Steve

reviews.author: Peter Zijlstra
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that adding an extra underscore to the function name provides no real benefit and is unnecessary
reviews.raw_body:
It already has __ prefix, no point in making the name even longer for no
real benefit.

reviews.author: Peter Zijlstra
reviews.summary: reviewer expressed concern that tracepoint instrumentation will be executed twice due to static branches not being optimized by compilers

reviews.author: Steven Rostedt
reviews.summary:
reviewer pointed out that the contended_release tracepoint instrumentation does not handle the case where a lock is released while still holding other locks, which could lead to incorrect tracing

reviewer suggested the patch is feasible and encouraged others to implement or review it
reviews.raw_body:
On Thu, 5 Mar 2026 18:42:23 +0100
Peter Zijlstra <peterz@infradead.org> wrote:

---

Makes sense, and that could probably be done. It shouldn't be too hard to
do. If I find some time I could look at it, or perhaps someone lurking on
this thread could possibly give it a try! (I may even Cc some people that
want to learn this code).

-- Steve

2026-03-05.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new tracepoint called contended_release that fires on the lock holder's side when a lock is released under contention. This allows for more accurate hold time estimation and provides the holder's stack at release time, which can differ from what is captured earlier in the locking process. The new tracepoint has minimal performance impact as it only affects slowpath unlock paths.
